title: chromolaena macrantha
intro: Chromolaena macrantha, also known as Tree Senna or Yellow Bells, is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and have been shown to have antioxidant and wound-healing properties, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair. Chromolaena macrantha can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in folk medicine to treat respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems.
